#+TITLE: How MATLAB Language Server and copilot.el Interact
#+AUTHOR: Auto-generated
#+OPTIONS: toc:2 num:t

* Overview

Based on your configuration, you have:
- *Eglot* connecting to the MATLAB language server for =matlab-ts-mode=
- *Corfu* for displaying LSP completions
- *copilot.el* for AI-powered ghost text completions

* They Work at Different Layers

#+begin_example
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    You Type Code                               │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              │
              ┌───────────────┴───────────────┐
              ▼                               ▼
┌─────────────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────────────────┐
│   MATLAB LSP (Eglot)    │     │      copilot.el             │
├─────────────────────────┤     ├─────────────────────────────┤
│ • Local analysis        │     │ • Cloud AI (GitHub)         │
│ • Fast, deterministic   │     │ • Slower, creative          │
│ • Symbol completions    │     │ • Line/block completions    │
│ • Function signatures   │     │ • Context-aware suggestions │
│ • Variable names        │     │ • Code generation           │
│ • Syntax checking       │     │ • Pattern completion        │
└─────────────────────────┘     └─────────────────────────────┘
              │                               │
              ▼                               ▼
┌─────────────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────────────────┐
│   Corfu Popup Menu      │     │   Ghost Text Overlay        │
│   (Manual: C-M-i)       │     │   (Automatic after delay)   │
└─────────────────────────┘     └─────────────────────────────┘
#+end_example

* What Each Provides

| Feature        | MATLAB LSP                      | copilot.el                        |
|----------------+---------------------------------+-----------------------------------|
| *Trigger*      | Manual (=C-M-i=) or after typing  | Automatic (after idle delay)      |
| *Display*      | Corfu popup with list           | Inline ghost text                 |
| *Completions*  | =myVariable=, =myFunction=          | Entire lines/blocks of code       |
| *Source*       | Static analysis of your code    | AI trained on code patterns       |
| *Speed*        | Fast (local)                    | Slower (network round-trip)       |
| *Scope*        | Current workspace symbols       | Contextual code generation        |

* Practical Example in MATLAB

Say you're writing a MATLAB file:

#+begin_src matlab
function result = processData(data)
    % Filter the data
    filtered = |  ← cursor here
#+end_src

** MATLAB LSP (via Corfu) would offer:
- =data= (the parameter)
- =filter= (built-in function)
- =filtfilt= (built-in function)
- Other symbols in scope

** copilot.el might suggest (as ghost text):
#+begin_src matlab
    filtered = data(data > 0);  % Remove negative values
#+end_src

* No Conflict - Different UI Elements

1. *Corfu popup* = A menu you navigate with =C-n=/=C-p=, select with =TAB=
2. *Copilot overlay* = Gray ghost text after cursor, accept with =TAB=

Your config already handles potential conflicts:

#+begin_src emacs-lisp
;; Disable copilot when Corfu popup is visible
(add-to-list 'copilot-disable-predicates
             (lambda () (and (boundp 'corfu--frame) corfu--frame)))
#+end_src

This means:
- When you trigger Corfu (=C-M-i=), copilot suggestions hide
- When Corfu closes, copilot suggestions can appear again

* Typical Workflow

| Task                                    | Best Tool                           |
|-----------------------------------------+-------------------------------------|
| Complete a variable name                | *LSP/Corfu* - knows exact symbols   |
| Complete a MATLAB built-in function     | *LSP/Corfu* - has full API          |
| Write a for-loop body                   | *copilot.el* - generates code       |
| Implement an algorithm from a comment   | *copilot.el* - understands intent   |
| Check function signature                | *LSP* - precise documentation       |
| Generate boilerplate code               | *copilot.el* - pattern matching     |

* Summary

They're *complementary, not competing*:

- *LSP* = Your precise, local expert on MATLAB syntax and your codebase
- *copilot.el* = Your AI assistant that suggests what to write next

Use LSP completions when you know /what/ you want but not the exact name. Use Copilot when you want the AI to /generate/ code based on context and comments.
